name: Gang Som Southern Fish Curry
intro: keep its shape after long cooking). The protein is a white-fl eshed fi sh; if you were to use three-layer

ingredients.item: fi rm white fi sh like cod, halibut or hake, skinned and cut into fi lets
Thai celery stalks, chopped (or the leaves of a celery plant)

method: In a blender or food processor, blitz the white fi sh until it becomes a fi ne paste. With a spatula, scrape down the sides and blitz again with the salt, tapioca, baking powder and pepper until well blended.

Transfer the fi sh paste to a cut- ting board. With wet hands, knead the fi sh paste well, slapping it onto the board a couple of times. This makes the paste springy. Wrap the paste in plastic wrap and put it in the freezer for 30 minutes.

After half an hour, take it out of the freezer. It should fi rmed up well. Wet your hands with some water and take a big spoonful of the fi sh paste onto your palm and shape into a ball. Repeat until all the fi sh paste is used up.
In a pot of barely boiling water, add the fi sh balls and boil them carefully for about 15 minutes.
They will puff up and double in size. Once cooked, put them in a strainer and stop the cooking pro- cess by dunking them in a bowl of iced water.
Drain and serve with your Thai seafood dipping sauce, a garnish of
Thai celery leaves and deep-fried garlic, and fresh coriander leaves.
